# Gmail Connector - Full Documentation

# Gmail Connector 1.1 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Gmail (Gmail Connector) enables the management of your Gmail account,
including sending and receiving emails and reacting to received emails.

## Default Configuration

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_Oauth2c">Oauth2c Connection Provider</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the response times out.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>MINUTES</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p><p>* <code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p><p>* <code>ALWAYS</code></p><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p><p>* <code>NEVER</code></p><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Connection Types

#### Oauth2c Connection Provider

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

##### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when initially establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p><p>* <code>true</code></p><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p><p>* <code>false</code></p><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections to open to the backend. HTTP requests are sent in parallel over multiple connections. Setting this value too high can impact latency and consume additional resources without increasing throughput.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Access type.</p></td><td><p><code>offline</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Asks for user consent even if the user is already logged in.</p></td><td><p><code>consent</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base Uri</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Parameter base URI. Each instance or tenant gets its own.</p></td><td><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. Valid values are <code>HTTP</code> and <code>HTTPS</code>.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs authorization endpoint.</p></td><td><p><code>https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs access token endpoint.</p></td><td><p><code>https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. This value defaults to the scopes in the annotation. Valid values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>&lt;Scope 1&gt;</p></li><li><p>&lt;Scope 2&gt;</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><code>https://mail.google.com/</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.addons.current.action.compose</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.addons.current.message.action</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.addons.current.message.metadata</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.addons.current.message.readonly</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.compose</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.insert</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.labels</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.metadata</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Resource owner ID to use with the authorization code grant type.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately after receiving an accessToken.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configuration for the HTTP listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [On New Email](#OnNewEmailListener)

## Create Draft

`<gmail:create-draft>`

Creates a new draft with the `DRAFT` label. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/drafts endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete Draft

`<gmail:gmailusersdraftsdelete>`

Immediately and permanently deletes the specified draft. It does not simply trash it. This operation
makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/drafts/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the draft to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Draft

`<gmail:gmailusersdraftsget>`

Gets the specified draft. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/drafts/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the draft to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MINIMAL</p></li><li><p>FULL</p></li><li><p>RAW</p></li><li><p>METADATA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Format to return the draft in.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## List Drafts

`<gmail:gmailusersdraftslist>`

Lists the drafts in the userâs mailbox. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/drafts endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of drafts to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Page token that retrieves a specific page of results in the list.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>q</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Return only draft messages that match the specified query. Supports the same query format as the Gmail search box. For example, <code>"from:someuser@example.com rfc822msgid: is:unread"</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Spam Trash</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Include drafts from <code>SPAM</code> and <code>TRASH</code> in the results.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY

## Send Draft

`<gmail:gmailusersdraftssend>`

Sends the specified, existing draft to the recipients in the `To`, `Cc`, and `Bcc` headers. This
operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/drafts/send endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT

- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Update Draft

`<gmail:gmailusersdraftsupdate>`

Replaces a draftâs content. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/drafts/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the draft to update.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Profile

`<gmail:gmailusersget-profile>`

Gets the current userâs Gmail profile. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/profile endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. - GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## List History

`<gmail:gmailusershistorylist>`

Lists the history of all changes to the given mailbox. History results are returned in chronological
order (increasing `historyId`). This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/history endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of history records to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Page token that retrieves a specific page of results in the list.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start History Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required. Returns history records after the specified <code>startHistoryId</code>. The supplied <code>startHistoryId</code> must be obtained from the <code>historyId</code> of a message, thread, or previous <code>list</code> response. History IDs increase chronologically but are not contiguous with random gaps in between valid IDs. Supplying an invalid or out of date <code>startHistoryId</code> typically returns an <code>HTTP 404</code> error code. A <code>historyId</code> is typically valid for at least a week, but in some rare circumstances may be valid for only a few hours. If you receive an <code>HTTP 404</code> error response, your application must perform a full sync. If you receive no <code>nextPageToken</code> in the response, there are no updates to retrieve and you can store the returned <code>historyId</code> for a future request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Label Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return messages with a label matching the ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>History Types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MESSAGE_ADDED</p></li><li><p>MESSAGE_DELETED</p></li><li><p>LABEL_ADDED</p></li><li><p>LABEL_REMOVED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>History types to return by the function.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create Label

`<gmail:gmailuserslabelscreate>`

Creates a new label. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/labels endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Delete Label

`<gmail:gmailuserslabelsdelete>`

Immediately and permanently deletes the specified label and removes it from any messages and threads
that it is applied to. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/labels/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the label to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR

- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND

## Get Label

`<gmail:gmailuserslabelsget>`

Gets the specified label. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/labels/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. ## List Labels

`<gmail:gmailuserslabelslist>`

Lists all of the labels in the userâs mailbox. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/labels endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. - GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Patch Label

`<gmail:gmailuserslabelspatch>`

Patch the specified label. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/labels/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the label to update.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Update Label

`<gmail:gmailuserslabelsupdate>`

Updates the specified label. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/labels/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Get Message Attachment

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagesattachmentsget>`

Gets the specified message attachment. Images that are attached to an email using the clip icon are
considered attachments, but images that are dragged into the body of an email are considered
embedded images and not attachments. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/{messageId}/attachments/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the message containing the attachment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the attachment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR

## Batch Delete Messages

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagesbatch-delete>`

Deletes many messages by message ID. Provides no guarantee that messages were not already deleted or
that those messages even existed at all. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/batchDelete endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY

- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND

## Batch Modify Messages

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagesbatch-modify>`
