## Delete Message

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagesdelete>`

Immediately and permanently deletes the specified message. This operation cannot be undone. This
operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the message to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Message

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagesget>`

Gets the specified message. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the message to retrieve. This ID is usually retrieved using <code>messages.list</code>. The ID is also contained in the result when a message is inserted (<code>messages.insert</code>) or imported (<code>messages.import</code>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MINIMAL</p></li><li><p>FULL</p></li><li><p>RAW</p></li><li><p>METADATA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The format to return the message in.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Metadata Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the format is <code>METADATA</code>, it includes only specified headers.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Import Message

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagesimport>`

Imports a message into a userâs mailbox only, with standard email delivery scanning and
classification similar to receiving via SMTP. Does not send a message. This function doesnât trigger
forwarding rules or filters set up by the user. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/import endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Internal Date Source</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RECEIVED_TIME</p></li><li><p>DATE_HEADER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Source for Gmailâs internal date of the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Never Mark Spam</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Ignore the Gmail spam classifier decision and never mark this email as SPAM in the mailbox.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Process For Calendar</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Process calendar invites in the email and add any extracted meetings to the Google Calendar for this user.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>deleted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Mark the email as permanently deleted (not TRASH) and visible only in Google Vault to a Vault administrator. Used only for G Suite accounts.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND

- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Insert Message

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagesinsert>`

Directly inserts a message into only this userâs mailbox similar to `IMAP APPEND`, bypassing most
scanning and classification. Does not send a message. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to
the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Internal Date Source</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RECEIVED_TIME</p></li><li><p>DATE_HEADER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Source for Gmailâs internal date of the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>deleted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Mark the email as permanently deleted (not TRASH) and visible only in Google Vault to a Vault administrator. Used only for G Suite accounts.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND

## List Messages

`<gmail:gmailusersmessageslist>`

Lists the messages in the userâs mailbox. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Label Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Return only messages with labels that match all of the specified label IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of messages to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Page token that retrieves a specific page of results in the list.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>q</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only return messages matching the specified query. Supports the same query format as the Gmail search box. For example, <code>"from:someuser@example.com rfc822msgid: is:unread"</code>. Parameter cannot be used when accessing the api using the gmail.metadata scope.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Spam Trash</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Include messages from <code>SPAM</code> and <code>TRASH</code> in the results.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Modify Message Labels

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagesmodify>`

Modifies the labels on the specified message. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/{id}/modify endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the message to modify.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR

- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND

## Send Message

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagessend>`

Sends the specified message to the recipients in the `To`, `Cc`, and `Bcc` headers. This operation
makes an HTTP POST request to the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/send endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Trash Message

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagestrash>`

Moves the specified message to the trash. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/{id}/trash endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Untrash Message

`<gmail:gmailusersmessagesuntrash>`

Removes the specified message from the trash. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/messages/{id}/untrash endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. - GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Add Account Delegate

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsdelegatescreate>`

Adds a delegate with its verification status set directly to `accepted`, without sending any
verification email. The delegate user must be a member of the same G Suite organization as the
delegator user. Gmail imposes limitations on the number of delegates and delegators each user in a G
Suite organization can have. These limits depend on your organization, but in general each user can
have up to 25 delegates and up to 10 delegators. A delegate user must be referred to by their
primary email address, and not an email alias. When a new delegate is created, there may be up to a
one minute delay before the new delegate is available for use. This method is available only to
service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority. This operation makes an HTTP
POST request to the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/delegates endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Remove Account Delegate

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsdelegatesdelete>`

Removes the specified delegate (which can be of any verification status), and revokes any
verification that may have been required for using it. A delegate user must be referred to by their
primary email address, and not an email alias. This method is available only to service account
clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request
to the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/delegates/{delegateEmail} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegate Email</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The email address of the user to remove as a delegate.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Account Delegate

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsdelegatesget>`

Gets the specified delegate. A delegate user must be referred to by their primary email address, and
not an email alias. This method is available only to service account clients that have been
delegated domain-wide authority. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/delegates/{delegateEmail} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegate Email</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The email address of the user whose delegate relationship is to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND

## List Account Delegates

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsdelegateslist>`

Lists the delegates for the specified account. This method is available only to service account
clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/delegates endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR

- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND

## Create Message Filter

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsfilterscreate>`

Creates a filter. You can create only a maximum of 1,000 filters. This operation makes an HTTP POST
request to the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/filters endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY

- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND

## Delete Message Filter

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsfiltersdelete>`

Deletes a filter. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/filters/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the filter to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Message Filter

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsfiltersget>`

Gets a filter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/filters/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the filter to fetch.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## List Message Filters

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsfilterslist>`

Lists the message filters of a Gmail user. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/filters endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Create New Fowarding Address

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsforwarding-addressescreate>`

Creates a forwarding address. If ownership verification is required, a message is sent to the
recipient and the resourceâs verification status will be set to `pending`. If not, the resource is
created with a verification status set to `accepted`. This method is available only to service
account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority. This operation makes an HTTP POST
request to the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/forwardingAddresses endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND

## Delete Forwarding Address

`<gmail:gmailuserssettingsforwarding-addressesdelete>`

Deletes the specified forwarding address and revokes any verification that may have been required.
This method is available only to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide
authority. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/settings/forwardingAddresses/{forwardingEmail} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Forwarding Email</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The forwarding address to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
