## Untrash Thread

`<gmail:gmailusersthreadsuntrash>`

Removes the specified thread from the trash. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/gmail/v1/users/{userId}/threads/{id}/untrash endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the thread to remove from Trash.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Setup Push Notifications

`<gmail:gmailuserswatch>`

Set up or update a push notification watch on the given user mailbox. This operation makes an HTTP
POST request to the /gmail/v1/users/{userId}/watch endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs email address. The special value <code>me</code> can be used to indicate the authenticated user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Response timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code>, the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- GMAIL:BAD_REQUEST
- GMAIL:CLIENT_ERROR
- GMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- GMAIL:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GMAIL:NOT_FOUND
- GMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GMAIL:SERVER_ERROR
- GMAIL:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GMAIL:TIMEOUT
- GMAIL:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GMAIL:UNAUTHORIZED
- GMAIL:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Unauthorize

`<gmail:unauthorize>`

Deletes all of the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that it is impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the resource owner which access should be invalidated.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

## On New Email

`<gmail:on-new-email-listener>`

Enters the flow when a new email is received.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Date that emails are polled. The format of the date is yyyy/mm/dd.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Search Query</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Search phrase, such as <code>in:label</code> or <code>from:foo@bar.com</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p>RedeliveryPolicy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

## Types

### Default Header

Headers automatically added to every outbound request.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key for this type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Value for this type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Default Query Param

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key for this type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Value for this type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy

Configures the settings used to connect through a proxy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname or IP address of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non Proxy Hosts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how to validate certificates.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Settig this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the &lt;b&gt;Prefer Crls&lt;/b&gt; field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certicate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector operation source or operation
attempts to reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often the Mule app attempts to reconnect to the remote service or API, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before
Mule considers it eligible for expiration.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### HTTP Response Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Status Code</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reason Phrase</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the memory stream. If the stream data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size value</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount by which the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower specifies that the buffer canât expand.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Buffer Unit</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Gmail Connector Overview](./)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Gmail Connector 1.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Gmail Reference](gmail-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Gmail Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the connectorâs
namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, type `gmail` in the search field.
3.  Click **Gmail** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met.

You can configure one of these sources to use with Gmail Connector:

- HTTP Listener  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- Scheduler  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP Listener source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform. To add an operation for Gmail Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Gmail** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the authentication credentials
that the connector requires to access the target Gmail system. Gmail Connector supports Oauth2c
authentication. To configure the global element for Gmail Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the [Oauth2c](#oauth2c) authentication method
    to configure:

    You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
    (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
    properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
    configure them, see
    [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

### Oauth2c

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure Oauth2c authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>User Action</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the configuration name.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Select <strong>Oauth2c</strong>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Listener Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration for the HTTP listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Callback Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorize Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path of the HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for Gmail, configure the other required fields for the
connector. The required fields vary depending on which connector operation you use.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output is visible in the Anypoint
  Studio console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output is visible in your
  OS console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`. You can configure the location of
the log path in the app log file `log4j2.xml`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Gmail Connector Reference](gmail-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Gmail Connector 1.1 - XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio:

- If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML either from the Anypoint Studio XML
  editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
  statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.
- If you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
  connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/gmail
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/gmail/current/mule-gmail.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML:

```xml
<mule xmlns:$2="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/gmail"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/gmail
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/$2/current/mule-$2.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
When you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access
to this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule4-gmail-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `<version>` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `Gmail`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Gmail Connector 1.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Gmail (Gmail Connector) enables the management of your Gmail account,
including sending and receiving emails and reacting to received emails.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, see the Gmail Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Gmail Connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the Gmail target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- Anypoint Studio version 7.0 or later

## Common Use Cases For the Connector

Some possible uses for this connector include:

- Sending an email to thank customers for a new purchase.
- Organizing all emails related to an event.
- Receiving attachments via email to verify an ID.

## Connection Types

Gmail connections use the Oauth2c authentication type, which uses access tokens provided by the
authorization server to access protected data.

For information about configuring authentication types, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Gmail Connector](gmail-connector-studio).

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, you are ready to create an app and configure the connector
using [Anypoint Studio](gmail-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Gmail Connector Reference](gmail-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
